description:
A built-in human participant that, when subscribed to an event, intercepts the negotiation outcome and stages it under .balls/local/pending-sync/{event}/{id}.json instead of committing immediately. Implements the "human gating desired on sync progress" story without the system enforcing anything.

## Mechanics

- bl sync --review invokes the participant in gating mode for the sync event. The would-be SyncReport is rendered to the operator and written to the staging dir; nothing is committed.
- bl sync apply <staged-id> commits the staged report through the normal participant apply path.
- bl sync discard <staged-id> drops it.
- Per-plugin config can subscribe the human participant to other events (claim, review, close) for stronger gating, off by default.

## Why it is a participant, not a special case

The same negotiation primitive runs; the human participant simply returns Staged instead of Ok or Err. No new dispatcher, no new code path. This is the proof that the participant model collapses what would otherwise be a parallel feature.

## Tests

- --review stages a populated SyncReport without mutating state-branch HEAD.
- apply replays the staged report and commits it normally.
- discard cleans up without side effects.
- A participant's failure during apply propagates per its own policy (independent of the human gate).
title: Human-gate participant: bl sync --review staged apply
